items.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 health intelligence upgrade and rare-disease diagnostic study, raising clinical AI expectations across UC Health
reasons: baseline_anchor field "has_health_ai_governance_forum" not present on ucop_systemwide.health_ai

raw.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 health intelligence upgrade and rare-disease diagnostic study, raising clinical AI expectations across UC Health
raw.what_happened: On June 18, 2026, OpenAI announced improvements to ChatGPT's health and wellness responses via GPT-5.5 Instant, emphasizing stronger clinical reasoning and physician-informed evaluations. Simultaneously, OpenAI published a study in which a reasoning model identified 18 new diagnoses in previously unsolved rare pediatric disease cases. Sources: openai.com/index/improving-health-intelligence-in-chatgpt and openai.com/index/diagnose-rare-childhood-diseases.
raw.why_it_matters: UC operates six health systems with varying degrees of clinical AI governance maturity. UCSF Health has the most developed oversight infrastructure — including a Chief Health AI Officer, the IMPACC continuous monitoring platform, and a Trustworthy AI framework — but even UCSF's Trustworthy AI principles do not yet address model-version changes in deployed enterprise tools. UC Health has a systemwide Health AI Governance Forum (CDI2, chaired by Cora Han) that coordinates monthly, but the baseline records no systemwide policy governing how mid-contract model upgrades to approved enterprise tools are evaluated for clinical safety before they reach deployed workflows. The rare-disease diagnostic result, while a research demonstration, will accelerate clinician demand for expanded LLM use in diagnostic settings across UC campuses where clinical AI governance is less mature than UCSF.
raw.for_the_committee: Does the UC Health AI Governance Forum have — or need — a standing protocol for evaluating vendor model-version upgrades to clinically deployed tools before those upgrades go live, and which health systems currently lack that capability?

feed_sources.title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children

experts.why: Chief Health Data Officer and chair of UC Health AI Governance Forum — directly responsible for cross-health-system coordination on clinical AI
UCSF Health CIO administering ChatGPT Enterprise in clinical context with P3/P4 data
